title: Megan Zhao
text:
BIO
Hello! My name is Megan. Currently, I am an undergraduate student at University of California, Santa Barbara, exploring the intersection between scientific ecology and developing policy. Feel free to explore, and learn a bit more about me and my work!



EDUCATION
 BS in Environmental Studies, 2028
University of California, Santa Barbara
 BA in Geography: Geographic Information Science (GIS), 2028
University of California, Santa Barbara
 BA in Philosophy: Ethics and Public Policy, 2028
University of California, Santa Barbara

title: About Me
text: A little more about me, both inside and outside of work!

title: About Me
text:
CURRENTLY
At the moment, I am seeking employment opportunities in conservation research and environmental policy. Since 2025, I have worked with Ken Hiltner at UCSB as an Undergraduate Research Assistant, approaching historical and modern media from an ecocritical perspective, and exploring the impacts our media has had on our modern environmental perceptions. Outside of UCSB, I have been conducting personal research about the ecological impacts of invasive shrew-species on island locales since 2023, specifically focusing on the Irish Isles and the effects nonnative greater-white-toothed shrews have had on the local ecology.

title: About Me
section: I’m always excited for the opportunity to collaborate on new/existing projects with others. Please feel free reach out if you are interested in connecting, working together, or even just to say hi!
